figures.caption: Table 1: Performance of MDM-50step and MotionLCM using only WIN retrieval on HumanML3D under generation with null prompts. Results are averaged over 20 evaluation runs with 95% confidence intervals.

Table 2: Performance of MDM-50step and MotionLCM with WINRO on HumanML3D under text-conditioned generation. MotionLCM uses 1-step inference. Results are averaged over 20 evaluation runs with 95% confidence intervals.
Table 3: Results on the MTT benchmark [27].

Table 6: Extended comparison of WINRO variants on HumanML3D. Retr.: initial noise selection from the noise dictionary—random Gaussian ( – ), Top-1, or Top-k (k=150); Refine: noise refinement—none ( – ), KL-regularized optimization (Optim), or the LoRA-based noise refiner (LoRA); Pert.: Gaussian perturbation of the final

Table 8: Ablation of WINRO refinement hyperparameters on MotionLCM. All runs start from the same WIN-FAR retrieval result and use 100 optimization steps. Each block varies one factor from the default setting (first row).
Fig. 12: Additional comparison of motions generated with random, retrieved, and refined initial noise for each text prompt, using MDM-50step.

Fig. 13: Additional compositional generation results on the multi-track timeline benchmark. STMC [27] often fails to realize individual action segments (e.g ., missing jump, punch, or hop), while applying our noise refinement (w/ Ours) improves faithfulness to each segment’s text prompt.
